design_philosophy.core_loop: Select team, compete in tournament or vs. matches by controlling one player and issuing pass/shoot commands to AI teammates; balance direct control with tactical positioning and resource management of limited super shots
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Real-time soccer action with asymmetric player control (controlling one of six players) creates tension between direct input and strategic delegation; limited super shots per half create resource scarcity tension
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Lead your national team to World Cup victory through skill and tactical decision-making; experience arcade soccer with physicality and environmental variety (ice, sand, dirt fields affect gameplay)
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Deliberately deviates from authentic soccer: no offsides, no foul punishments, players can be permanently knocked out per half, small rosters (6v6), heavy emphasis on super shots as power fantasy moments. This prioritizes arcade action spectacle over simulation, making soccer about direct confrontation and special abilities rather than positional play

difficulty.checkpointing: Tournament mode progresses through tournament brackets; knocked out players remain down for rest of half but respawn next half
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High agency: players select from 13 national teams with distinct super shot mechanics and stats; can play tournament or vs. match modes; field surface variations (ice slows movement) affect difficulty dynamically

progression.upgrade_system: Each team has distinct super shot mechanics that differ per half (up to five uses per half); field surfaces vary match-to-match in tournament progression
progression.unlock_mechanism: Tournament mode plays through bracket of 13 teams; PC Engine Super CD-ROM² version unlocks additional national teams after winning final match
replayability_hooks: 13 distinct national teams with unique super shot mechanics encourage replaying with different teams
VS. Match mode with three field surface options (sand, ice, dirt) allows custom matchups
Multi-player support (up to 4 players on NES) shifts from PvE to PvP replayability
Different team compositions and super shot strategies create tactical variety

world_building_techniques: Regional localization: Famicom version centered on Japanese high school tournament; NES version recontextualized as international World Cup
Team rosters reused character sprites from River City Ransom (Kunio-kun series continuity)
13 national teams with distinct visual identities and super shot animations provide cultural flavor

faction_indicators: 13 national teams (Argentina, Brazil, Cameroon, England, France, West Germany, Italy, Japan, Mexico, Netherlands, Soviet Union, Spain, United States) represent geopolitical affiliations
Kunio and Nekketsu High School team appear as Japanese representatives, linking to Downtown Nekketsu Story/River City Ransom universe
danger_signals: Physical knockouts: players can be permanently disabled per half through repeated tackling, escalating match violence
No foul system: rules-breaking is mechanically encouraged and unrestricted
environmental_storytelling: Field surface variations (ice, sand, dirt) create distinct play environments with world-specific physics
Team sprite designs and super shot visual effects carry cultural identity markers
